entries.title: SOLID warm-up — UserManager violation
entries.question: UserManager handles login, sends welcome emails, and writes to DB. Which SOLID principle does this most clearly violate?
entries.answer: Single Responsibility Principle — the class has more than one reason to change
entries.explanation: UserManager has three separate reasons to change: auth logic, email templates, and DB schema. SRP says a class should have only one reason to change.

entries.title: Implement INotifier — SMSNotifier & EmailNotifier
entries.question: Implement SMSNotifier and EmailNotifier satisfying INotifier. Write a notify() free function that accepts INotifier& and calls send().
entries.answer: Two concrete classes inheriting INotifier, overriding send() with correct const std::string& signature and override keyword. notify() accepts INotifier& — decoupled from concrete type.
entries.explanation: override keyword enforces signature match at compile time. const& on parameters must match the interface exactly. The free function notify(INotifier&) demonstrates low coupling — caller doesn't know or care which notifier it receives.
entries.content: // Student dropped const, missing override, missing std::, msg not printed in output
entries.feedback: Design intent correct. Three C++ hygiene issues: missing override, missing const on parameter, message not forwarded to output. All caught by compiler with override keyword.
